name: Linux Commands
description: Essential Linux commands, flags, and concepts for the wargame and general Linux fundamentals

cards.back: Lists directory contents. Common flags: -l (long format), -a (show hidden), -h (human-readable sizes), -R (recursive), -t (sort by time).
Change directory. `cd ~` or `cd` = home, `cd -` = previous dir, `cd ..` = parent dir, `cd /` = root.
Concatenate and print file contents to stdout. `cat file1 file2` merges output. `-n` adds line numbers, `-A` shows non-printing chars.
Search text using patterns/regex. `-i` case insensitive, `-r` recursive, `-n` line numbers, `-v` invert match, `-c` count matches, `-l` filenames only.
Search for files in a directory hierarchy. `-name` by name, `-type f/d` by type, `-size` by size, `-mtime` by modification time, `-exec` run command on results.
Change file permissions. Numeric: `chmod 755 file` (rwxr-xr-x). Symbolic: `chmod u+x file`. Digits: owner/group/others. 4=read, 2=write, 1=execute.
Change file owner and group. `chown user:group file`. `-R` for recursive. Only root can change ownership.
Secure Shell — remote login over encrypted connection. `ssh user@host`. `-p` port, `-i` identity file (private key), `-L` local port forward, `-D` SOCKS proxy.
Secure copy over SSH. `scp file user@host:/path`. `-r` recursive (directories), `-P` port. Copies files between local and remote machines securely.
Transfer data with URLs. `-o` output file, `-O` save as remote name, `-L` follow redirects, `-H` add header, `-X` HTTP method, `-d` POST data, `-s` silent.
Non-interactive network downloader. `-O` output file, `-r` recursive, `-c` continue partial download, `-q` quiet, `--mirror` mirror a site.
Archive files. Create: `tar -czf archive.tar.gz dir/`. Extract: `tar -xzf archive.tar.gz`. `-c` create, `-x` extract, `-z` gzip, `-j` bzip2, `-f` file, `-v` verbose.
Report running processes. `ps aux` = all processes with details. `ps -ef` = full format. Shows PID, user, CPU%, memory%, command.
Send signal to a process. `kill PID` sends SIGTERM (15). `kill -9 PID` sends SIGKILL (force). `kill -l` lists all signals. `killall name` kills by name.
Real-time process monitor. Shows CPU, memory, load average, per-process stats. `htop` is the interactive/colorful version. Press `q` to quit, `k` to kill.
Execute command as superuser (or another user). `sudo -u user cmd`. `sudo -i` = root shell. `sudo !!` = rerun last command as root. Config in /etc/sudoers.
Display manual pages. `man command`. Navigate: arrows/PgUp/PgDn. Search: `/pattern`. Sections: 1=commands, 5=file formats, 8=admin commands.
Sends stdout of one command to stdin of another. Example: `cat file | grep pattern | sort | uniq -c`. Chains commands for data processing.
`>` redirects stdout to a file (overwrites). `>>` appends. `2>` redirects stderr. `&>` or `2>&1` redirects both stdout and stderr.
Pattern scanning and text processing language. `awk '{print $1}' file` prints first field. `-F:` sets delimiter. Supports conditions, loops, and built-in variables.
Stream editor for text transformation. `sed 's/old/new/g' file` substitutes. `-i` edits in place. `d` deletes lines. Supports regex and line addressing.
`head -n 20 file` shows first 20 lines. `tail -n 20 file` shows last 20. `tail -f file` follows a file in real-time (great for logs).
Sort lines of text. `-r` reverse, `-n` numeric sort, `-k2` sort by field 2, `-u` unique, `-t` delimiter. Often piped: `| sort -rn`.
Filter adjacent duplicate lines. Usually used after sort. `-c` count occurrences, `-d` only duplicates, `-u` only unique. `sort file | uniq -c | sort -rn` = frequency count.
Word count. `-l` lines, `-w` words, `-c` bytes, `-m` characters. `wc -l file` counts lines. Often piped: `cat file | wc -l`.
Compare files line by line. Shows additions/deletions. `-u` unified format (like git diff), `-r` recursive for directories, `-q` just report if different.
Determine file type by examining content (not extension). `file mystery` might output 'ELF 64-bit executable' or 'ASCII text'. Uses magic number database.
Extract printable strings from binary files. Useful for finding text in executables, passwords in data files. `-n 8` = minimum 8 chars.
Create hex dump of a file. `xxd file` shows hex + ASCII. `xxd -r` reverses hex dump back to binary. Useful for binary file inspection.
Encode/decode base64. `base64 file` encodes. `base64 -d` decodes. `echo text | base64` encodes string. Used for safely transmitting binary data as text.
Network Swiss Army knife. Connect to ports: `nc host port`. Listen: `nc -l port`. Transfer files, port scan (`-z`), chat, reverse shells. TCP/UDP.
Network scanner. Port scanning, service detection, OS fingerprinting. `nmap -sV host` (version detection), `-sS` SYN scan, `-p-` all ports, `-A` aggressive scan.
Crypto toolkit. `openssl s_client -connect host:443` tests TLS. `openssl enc -aes-256-cbc` encrypts. Generate keys, CSRs, certificates, hash functions.
Generate SSH key pairs. `ssh-keygen -t ed25519` (recommended). Creates ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 (private) and .pub (public). `-b 4096` for RSA key size.
Display environment variables. `env` lists all. `printenv PATH` shows specific var. Set with `export VAR=value`. Unset with `unset VAR`.
Make a variable available to child processes. `export PATH=$PATH:/new/dir`. Without export, variable is only in current shell. Put in ~/.bashrc for persistence.
Schedule recurring tasks. `crontab -e` edit, `-l` list. Format: `min hour dom month dow command`. `*/5 * * * *` = every 5 minutes. Logs in /var/log/cron.
Control systemd services. `start/stop/restart/status service`. `enable/disable` for boot. `list-units` shows running. `journalctl -u service` for logs.
Query systemd journal logs. `-u service` filter by unit. `-f` follow. `--since today`. `-b` current boot. `-p err` priority filter. Replaces reading /var/log/* directly.
`mount /dev/sda1 /mnt` attaches filesystem. `umount /mnt` detaches. `mount` alone shows mounted filesystems. /etc/fstab defines auto-mounts at boot.
Disk free — show filesystem space usage. `-h` human-readable. `-i` inode usage. Shows mount point, size, used, available, use%.
Disk usage — estimate file/directory space. `-h` human-readable, `-s` summary (total only), `--max-depth=1` one level. `du -sh *` = size of each item.
Create links. `ln -s target link` = symbolic (soft) link. `ln target link` = hard link. Symlinks can cross filesystems; hard links cannot and share inodes.
Build command lines from stdin. `find . -name '*.log' | xargs rm`. `-I{}` placeholder: `| xargs -I{} cp {} /backup/`. `-P4` parallel execution.
Read stdin, write to stdout AND files simultaneously. `cmd | tee output.log` shows output and saves it. `-a` appends instead of overwriting.
Extract sections from lines. `-d:` delimiter, `-f1,3` fields 1 and 3. `cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd` = usernames. `-c1-10` = character positions.
Translate or delete characters. `tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'` uppercases. `tr -d '\n'` removes newlines. `tr -s ' '` squeezes repeated spaces. Reads from stdin only.
Create command shortcuts. `alias ll='ls -la'`. `unalias ll` removes it. Put in ~/.bashrc for persistence. `alias` alone lists all current aliases.
setuid: file executes as file owner (e.g., /usr/bin/passwd runs as root). setgid: file executes as file group. On directories, setgid makes new files inherit group.
List open files. `lsof -i :80` = what's using port 80. `lsof -u user` = files opened by user. `lsof +D /dir` = files open in directory. Everything is a file in Linux.
